title: One command deleted {removed} duplicate rows in {wall} seconds
search_intent: fastest way to remove duplicate rows from a csv without excel
task_definition: TASK: clean a messy 1,000-row contact CSV
proof_plan: Run mlr clean-whitespace + uniq on a synthetic messy fixture in CI; record the pty session, wall time, and row counts; replay the real bytes on screen.

script.input: This contact list has {n_in} rows. Duplicates, random capitals, stray spaces.
script.proof: One free command. Miller cleans the whitespace and drops every duplicate.
script.output: Done in {wall} seconds. {removed} junk rows gone, {n_out} clean contacts left.
script.verdict: Verdict: it works. Save this for your next messy export. What file should I stress-test next?

verdict_lines: Save this for your next messy export.
Next: the same task, but with AI.

capture.shell_line: mlr --csv clean-whitespace then uniq -a messy_contacts.csv > clean_contacts.csv && wc -l *.csv

description_note: Real run: the terminal output, timing, and row counts in this video come from an actual recorded execution (proof ledger in the pipeline). Narration is AI text-to-speech.
